title: search_oracc data payload
description: Structured payload for the `search_oracc` tool. UPenn ORACC mirror's HTML pager scraped into typed hits. The `total_hits` field comes from `data-imax` on the page wrapper and reports the upstream count; `hits` is the parsed subset (some pages report hits we can't parse because of unusual markup, surfaced via the `unparsed_hits` count).

project.description: ORACC project code as a slash-path. Top-level (e.g. `dcclt`) or nested (e.g. `saao/saa01`).

total_hits.description: Total hits as reported by `data-imax` on the page response (NOT necessarily the count of `hits` returned).

unique_texts.description: Number of distinct text IDs among the parsed hits.

unparsed_hits.description: When `total_hits > len(hits) + offsets`, the gap is hits the regex didn't recognize on this page.

text_id.description: P/Q/X-prefixed text identifier extracted from the iref.

iref.description: Full `data-iref` attribute value as ORACC stored it — useful for deeplinks.

citation.description: Canonical publication citation as rendered on the pager.

snippet.description: Stripped text of the matched line. Tag-marked highlights are not preserved — see `rendered` in the envelope for the markdown form.

hit_type.description: Which result shape produced this hit. ORACC uses `refline tr` for translation hits and `ce-result` for transliteration hits.
